# Less is More: 4 Design Patterns for Building Better MCP Servers > Source excerpt preserved for provenance. Do not edit this file. > Compiled into wiki articles during the ingest stage. --- ## Core Principle "Minimizing context window usage while maximizing relevant information delivery" is crucial for effective MCP design. Current tools already consume significant context — Cursor and Claude Code expose 18 and 15 tools respectively, consuming "5.9% of context window before any user prompt" begins. ## The Four Design Patterns ### 1. Semantic Search Build a vector similarity search layer above your tool catalog, dynamically retrieving relevant tools based on user queries rather than exposing all tools upfront.
